body:
status=open
reason=Bash tool is completely unusable in this execution worktree because the host is out of disk space (ENOSPC). Every Bash invocation — including a bare `echo hi` — fails before running any command with: "ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-66c4070a-20260708T202211-1f012f8a'". This blocks `go build`, `go test`, `git status/add/commit`, and `lefthook run pre-commit` — every verification and commit step this bead's acceptance criteria require. Read/Write tools still work (they don't need the Bash tool's per-session tmp scratch dir), so I confirmed the repo state and the relevant source files (cli/internal/agent/tracker_sync.go, execute_bead_loop.go) are readable, but I cannot run any shell command to implement, compile, test, stage, or commit changes. This is a host/infrastructure blocker (disk full under /tmp), not a code or design issue, and is outside this bead's scope to resolve (no shell access to clean up /tmp or free disk space). Retrying the identical no-op Bash command reproduced the same ENOSPC failure every time, confirming this is not transient contention between parallel calls. Needed before this bead can proceed: free disk space on the host (or a fresh worktree/session with a healthy /tmp) so the Bash tool can initialize.
suggested_action=decompose into executable follow-up work or retry with a stronger agent

no_changes
